MIXED
BERRY
JAM

Makes approximately 800ml (enough for 3-4 jars)
Preparation time: 20 minutes | Cooking time: 45 minutes

INGREDIENTS:
400g blackberries
300g raspberries
250g blueberries
650g sugar (or jam sugar with pectin)
Juice of 1 lemon
1 vanilla pod, split (optional)
Pinch of sea salt

EQUIPMENT:
Sterilized jam jars
Muslin cloth
Wooden spoon
Sugar thermometer (helpful but not essential)

METHOD:
1. Combine berries, sugar, and lemon juice in a large, heavy-bottomed pot.
2. Heat gently, stirring occasionally, until sugar dissolves completely.
3. Bring to a rolling boil. Add vanilla pod seeds if using.
4. Maintain boil for 30-40 minutes, stirring frequently. Skim foam as it rises.
5. Test for setting point: place small spoonful on a cold plate. Push with finger—
if it wrinkles, jam is ready. Temperature should reach 104°C.
6. Remove from heat. Let cool 5 minutes before pouring into sterilized jars.
7. Seal immediately. Label with date and berry varieties.

STORAGE: Keeps for up to 18 months in cool, dark place. Refrigerate after opening.

NOTES: Do not skip lemon juice—it provides acid and helps jam set. For chunky texture,
add berries in stages. For smooth jam, pass through sieve after cooking.
